readings.title: Roofline: An Insightful Visual Performance Model for Multicore Architectures
readings.authors: Samuel Williams, Andrew Waterman, David Patterson
readings.description: The foundational 2009 paper introducing the Roofline model for understanding compute-bound vs. memory-bound performance. Essential for understanding why matrix multiplication is compute-bound and why tiling works. Published in Communications of the ACM.

readings.title: Anatomy of High-Performance Matrix Multiplication
readings.authors: Kazushige Goto, Robert A. van de Geijn
readings.description: The classic paper explaining how production BLAS libraries (GotoBLAS) achieve near-peak performance for GEMM through multi-level tiling, register blocking, and micro-kernels. The techniques described here are still used in OpenBLAS.

readings.title: Intel Intrinsics Guide
readings.description: Interactive reference for all x86 SIMD intrinsics (SSE, AVX, AVX-512, AMX). Searchable by operation, data type, and instruction set. Essential when writing SIMD code with intrinsics.

readings.title: ARM SVE/SVE2 Programmer's Guide
readings.description: Official guide to ARM's Scalable Vector Extension, covering vector-length-agnostic programming, predicate registers, and the whilelt pattern for loop control.

readings.title: Gaussian Elimination is Not Optimal (Strassen's Original Paper)
readings.description: The 1969 paper where Strassen proved that matrix multiplication can be done in fewer than n^3 multiplications. A landmark result in computational complexity that opened the field of fast matrix multiplication algorithms. Published in Numerische Mathematik.

readings.title: In-Datacenter Performance Analysis of a Tensor Processing Unit
readings.description: Google's paper describing the TPU v1 architecture, including its 256x256 systolic array, 8-bit integer MAC units, and performance comparison with contemporary CPUs and GPUs. Presented at ISCA 2017.
